sections.paragraph: m. (denom. from מֶלַח salt), halimus, Greek ἅλιμος (atriplex halimus, Linn.), orach, sea-purslain, a saline plant (compare the word salad, as used in Engl. also Ital., French, Germ.), the leaves of this plant, both raw and dressed, were eaten by the poor (Athen. Deipnos. iv. 16), Job 30:4 see Abenbitar ap. Boch. in Hieroz. t. i. p. 873, seqq.
